findings.title: Deploy script can broadcast Base router configuration on the wrong chain
findings.suggested_fix: Before vm.startBroadcast, read BASE_CHAIN_ID and revert if block.chainid does not match. Add a deployment-script test that exercises a non-Base chain id and proves no deploy/configuration broadcast path can proceed with Base venue targets on the wrong RPC.

findings.title: Live validation can pass with stale executable routes because it only checks current pool metadata
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the release validation gate to materialize routes with fresh near-term deadlines and quote-derived per-leg/final minimums, then simulate or execute every launch route against current Base state. Keep pinned fork execution as regression evidence, but fail release validation when current liquidity or Universal Router behavior no longer satisfies the route set.

findings.title: Fork-evidence artifacts include submit-able calldata with year-2100 deadlines and one-wei minimums
findings.suggested_fix: Move raw abiEncodedRoute values for productionExecutable=false artifacts into a test-only fixture, rename them to testAbiEncodedRoute, or require all consumers to reject productionExecutable=false before exposing calldata. Production paths should only emit freshly materialized routes with caller-supplied recipient, near-term deadline, and quote-derived minimums.

findings.title: Native ETH leftover refunds can revert valid routes from non-payable contract senders
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit refund recipient, wrap native leftovers to WETH before refunding, or reject route shapes that can leave route-local native ETH when msg.sender cannot receive ETH. Cover a non-payable contract sender with a native-leftover route in tests.

residual_risks: Read-only review only; I did not run Foundry, Bun, deployment, or Base fork validation commands because the request constrained shell commands to read-only.

I did not find hardcoded private keys, mnemonics, raw RPC URLs, explorer keys, or bearer tokens in the inspected public config and broadcast artifacts; broadcast files do expose public deployer addresses, transaction calldata, transaction hashes, chain id 8453, and deployed router addresses.

Universal Router V3/V4 reliability still depends on live Permit2 and Universal Router semantics at the configured Base target; the fork suite is the meaningful proof and should run with Doppler-provided BASE_RPC before release.
testing_gaps: No deployment-script test proves DeployFameRouter refuses to broadcast when the connected RPC chain id differs from BASE_CHAIN_ID.
No release validation test fails when fixture hashes and live pool metadata match but current route execution or quote-derived minimums fail.
No consumer-facing test rejects productionExecutable=false fork-evidence artifacts before calldata can be submitted.
No non-payable contract-sender test covers native ETH route-local leftover refund behavior.
